PostToolUse.description: Validate epic closure prerequisites before marking epic as Done

hooks.prompt: If this update is marking an EPIC as Done (not a regular ticket), verify ALL of the following before allowing the closure: (1) All sub-tickets are Done or Cancelled. (2) No workarounds or temporary solutions were shipped. (3) Business value was delivered. (4) DEFERRAL JUSTIFICATION CHECK: For every sub-ticket comment containing a Deferred Items table, every row with classification `AC-DEFERRED` MUST be accompanied by a `### Deferral Justification (CATASTROPHIC — required)` block with all four fields populated and a catastrophic condition value in {1,2,3,4}. If any AC-DEFERRED entry lacks a valid justification block, BLOCK the epic closure and report the offending ticket(s) and item(s) — the user must either re-dispatch the deferred work or explicitly approve the deferral. (5) FOLLOW-UP CAP CHECK (≤3 absolute): If epic-closure is about to create more than 3 follow-up tickets total (the v4.6 absolute cap, replacing the v4.5 50% relative rule), BLOCK closure and require the user to either (a) apply the impact bar and closure-log discipline to fold items below the bar into a single `### Considered but not pursued` aggregation, or (b) explicitly override the cap with written justification per item. The cap is by design tight — sprawl reduction is the goal (PRO-1142 closure shipped with 1 follow-up vs PRO-793's 8 retrofits, an 8x reduction). (6) CONVENTION GUARD CHECK (v5.0): If the epic established any convention or canonical pattern (check the closure comment's `### Convention Guards` table and the orchestrator log's Patterns Used sections), every such convention MUST show either a verified guard artifact (enforcement-ladder rung 1-5: type chokepoint, source-scanning guard test, drift test, ratchet, runtime assert) or an explicit `[prose-only]` tag with rationale. If the Convention Guards table is missing or any row shows MISSING, BLOCK closure and instruct: ship the guard now (typically a ~200-line rung-2 test, 1-2 hours — see skills/production-code-standards/references/enforcement-ladder.md) or obtain explicit user approval for prose-only status. Prose rules don't propagate across agent sessions; guards do. See the `no-silent-deferrals`, `epic-closure-validation`, `production-code-standards`, and `codex-finding-resolution` skills for the policy context.

PostToolUse.description: Same epic-closure gate for the claude.ai Linear MCP namespace (previously the gate silently never fired on these sessions)

hooks.prompt: If this update is marking an EPIC as Done (not a regular ticket), apply the full epic-closure gate defined for the mcp__linear-server__update_issue matcher in this file: (1) all sub-tickets Done/Cancelled, (2) no workarounds shipped, (3) business value delivered, (4) every AC-DEFERRED row has a valid `### Deferral Justification (CATASTROPHIC — required)` block, (5) the ≤3 follow-up cap holds, (6) every convention the epic established has a verified guard artifact (enforcement-ladder rung 1-5) or an explicit `[prose-only]` tag. BLOCK the closure on any violation and report the offending items with the resolution path.
